questions.question: Has the step-2 prerequisite already landed in the intended execution branch so live messages patch the existing `useMarket(marketId)` query, or would this step also need to backfill missing subscription/cache-update plumbing before `MarketDetail` can stay on `const m = data.market`?
questions.why_it_matters: This checkout still exposes only REST hooks in `frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts`, while step 3 assumes the route can remain query-driven and consume live updates indirectly through the existing market query.

questions.question: For this step, which queries are considered part of the route's required query-driven state: only `useMarket(...)`, or also `useMarketEvents`, `useEngineStats`, and `useMarkets` that feed adjacent panels on the detail page?
questions.why_it_matters: The route renders more than `ProbabilityBar`. If only `data.market` stays fresh while the event journal, junction-tree panel, or assumptions market list lag behind, the page is not fully query-driven in practice.

questions.question: When a live update moves the market from `active` to `resolved` or `closed`, is the intended behavior simply to let the patched `data.market.status` rerender hide active-only surfaces, or are any extra teardown/refetch side effects expected at the same moment?
questions.why_it_matters: The route already gates `AssumptionPanel` directly on `m.status`, and `EventTradePanel` and `ResolveMarketPanel` also branch on `market.status`. Whether that is sufficient determines if this step stays route-thin or needs broader coordination.

questions.question: Should disconnect and reconnect behavior remain entirely inside the subscription/query layer, with `MarketDetail` continuing to render whatever React Query last has from REST, or is the route expected to surface connection-state UI or perform explicit fallback work?
questions.why_it_matters: The step title says to fall back to the current REST query state without adding route-local reset logic. That still leaves an open boundary around whether the route should know about socket state at all.

questions.question: Are child-component local states intentionally out of scope for this step, or should any of them reset on live sequence/status changes even though the route itself must not add local reset logic?
questions.why_it_matters: `EventTradePanel` and `ResolveMarketPanel` keep local selections, confirmation state, and mutation banners that are independent of the query cache, so the exact scope line matters before implementation.

questions.question: What evidence is expected to prove the route stayed query-driven after this step: route tests that assert terminal updates hide active-only UI through `data.market`, tests for disconnect fallback preserving REST-backed rendering, or reliance on lower-level hook coverage?
questions.why_it_matters: The current `MarketDetail` Vitest coverage is REST-only. Clarifying the verification boundary prevents this step from either under-testing the route contract or duplicating hook-level websocket tests.

assumptions.assumption: Step 3 is supposed to consume step-2 cache-patching work rather than introduce any route-local market mirror or direct socket message handling inside `MarketDetail`.
assumptions.if_wrong: The implementation scope is larger than the step title suggests and this question set understates missing prerequisite work.

assumptions.assumption: Once `data.market.status` changes, the existing status gates in `MarketDetail`, `EventTradePanel`, and `ResolveMarketPanel` are intended to hide active-only surfaces without extra route cleanup.
assumptions.if_wrong: The route or child panels will need additional explicit teardown behavior, which conflicts with the current 'no route-local reset logic' framing.

assumptions.assumption: Disconnect/reconnect fallback means React Query's current REST-backed data remains the render source of truth, and any reconnect/refetch mechanics belong in the subscription hook rather than in route state.
assumptions.if_wrong: The route may need to subscribe to connection status, add fallback UI, or coordinate explicit resets when the socket drops.

assumptions.assumption: Child local selections and success/error banners are acceptable to persist until rerender or unmount for this step; only correctness of cache-driven rendering is in scope.
assumptions.if_wrong: Component-level reset requirements will need to be pulled into the implementation or split into follow-up work.
